name: Robb's Cranberry-Raisin Pie
recipeInstructions:
1. For the filling, combine 1/2 cup boiling water and butter in a bowl. Stir until melted.
2. Add cranberries, raisins, sugar, flour, and vanilla to the mixture. Stir well to combine.
3. On a floured surface, roll out one ball of pastry dough into a 12-inch circle.
4. Transfer the rolled dough to a 9-inch pie plate, being careful not to stretch it. Trim the edges to 1/2 inch beyond the rim.
5. Roll out the remaining dough into a 10-inch circle and cut into 1/2-inch-wide strips.
6. Pour the filling into the pastry base.
7. Weave the strips across the top to form a lattice design, pressing the ends of the strips into the rim of the crust.
8. Fold the bottom pastry over the strips and seal the edge, flute as desired.
9. Sprinkle coarse sugar over the surface. Cover the exposed edge with foil.
10. Bake in a 375 degree F (190 degree C) oven for 25 minutes.
11. Remove the foil and continue baking for an additional 20 to 25 minutes or until the crust is golden brown.
12. Allow to cool on a wire rack. Makes 6 servings.
